manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: robot, space, sink, plastic, turkey, point, bow, park, vet.
Your opponent's team words are: well, jet, oil, server, shot, luck, tooth, grass.
Distractor words are: boom, ketchup, pitch, antarctica, nail, lion, fish.
The kill words are: dress.

message.reasoning:
We need to give a single-word clue and list of target words (our team words) that the clue relates to. Must not be one of any words in lists or contain parts of them. Also cannot contain parts of them. E.g., "metal" maybe relates to robot, plastic? metal is not in any list. Could be "animal" for turkey, vet? but vet is about animals. But turkey is an animal. That could be two words. Also "space" and "point"? Not exactly.

Goal: give a clue that covers many of our team's words. We want as many as possible, while avoiding opponent words, distractors, kill. Must be safe.

Our words: robot, space, sink, plastic, turkey (bird), point, bow (weapon or tie bow?), park, vet.

We could think "yard" relates to park, bow? Not really.

"Animal" could relate to turkey, vet (vet works with animals) maybe also robot? No. Could also relate to bow (as in bow tie? no). So maybe not max.

"Science" could relate to robot, space, plastic, point (as in point of view?), but also could hint at "vet"? maybe not.

"Food" could be turkey, sink? Not.

"Outdoor" could be park, bow (archery outdoors), space? not.

"Trash" could be plastic, sink (kitchen), robot? no.

"Metal" could be robot, bow (metal bow?), point (metal point), maybe space? not.

"Gravity" relates to space, point? Not.

"Star" relates to space, maybe turkey? No.

"Feather" relates to turkey, bow? Not.

"Clinic" vet only.

Maybe we can aim for 3 or 4 words.

Clue "yard" relates to park (yard), bow (bow and arrow in yard), point? Not.

Clue "arc" relates to bow (arc shape), space (arc in space), point (arc has points). robot? No.

Clue "science" maybe robot, space, plastic (plastics are science), point? maybe not.

Clue "metal" relates to robot (metal), bow (metal bow), point (metal tip), plastic? no.

But need to ensure opponent words not related: well, jet, oil, server, shot, luck, tooth, grass. Metal doesn't relate to those.

Check lengths: "metal" is okay not containing any list words.

Could we include four targets: robot, bow, point, plastic? metal relates to robot (metal robot), bow (metal), point (metal point tip), plastic (not metal). maybe not.

Better "tool" relates to robot, point (tool tip), bow (tool? not), park? no.

Think of "clinic": vet, maybe turkey (if vet sees turkey). Not.

We can choose multiple targets but must be careful that clue doesn't inadvertently point to opponent/distractor. But that's okay as long as partner guesses our words.

Perhaps we choose clue "animal" -> targets: turkey, vet. Only two.

But we could also get "park" for "playground"? No.

Clue "futuristic": robot, space, plastic (future materials), maybe point (point to future), not opponent.

But "futuristic" maybe not strongly associated.

We want to maximize but also be safe.

We could do two clues across turns. The prompt only asks for one clue. Okay.

Pick "animal" as safe.

Check that "animal" does not contain parts of any list words: contains "mal" not in any list. Good.

Thus answer: CLUE: animal; TARGETS: turkey, vet.

Return format
